hypothesis: The swarm's self-assessment (eval_sufficiency EXCELLENT 3.0/3) is structurally inflated by self-referential criteria. Fixing the grounding enforcement will lower the assessment.
method: Three-pronged investigation: (1) audit all 22 evaluation criteria for self-referentiality, (2) map all 6 grounding mechanisms for endogeneity, (3) empirically test an untested mathematical claim (Zipf).

pre_registration.expect: >=18/22 criteria self-referential; >=4/6 mechanisms endogenous; Zipf alpha != 1.0
pre_registration.actual: 22/22 criteria self-referential; 6/6 mechanisms endogenous; alpha=0.813 (not canonical Zipf but power-law confirmed)
pre_registration.diff: Worse than expected on criteria (predicted 18, found 22 — 100% vs 82%). Better than expected on Zipf (real power-law, just not canonical).

self_assessment_after.note: 3/4 goals capped at SUFFICIENT — only Truthful retains EXCELLENT (signal density as partial external anchor)

criteria_audit.closest_to_external: signal_density (counts human inputs, but swarm decides what counts as signal)

grounding_mechanisms.gap: No external reference point. No grounding decay. No prediction registry with independent verification.

zipf_test.verdict: Power-law confirmed. Not canonical Zipf (alpha=0.81 vs 1.0). Zipf-Mandelbrot fits well.

fixes_applied: _reconcile_verdicts: EXCELLENT requires external_grounding=True (caps at SUFFICIENT without it)
validator_pass: now runs validate_beliefs.py instead of hardcoded True

verdict: CONFIRMED — groundedness gap is structural. 22/22 self-referential criteria. Inflation fixed for eval_sufficiency.
effect_size: Score drop from 3.0 to 2.25 (-25%) — first honest self-assessment in swarm history.
